user.title: Petlibro Local Setup
user.description: Choose how to set up your Petlibro feeder for local control.

auto_detect_dns.title: DNS Redirect Setup
auto_detect_dns.description:
To capture your feeder's credentials, you need to temporarily redirect its MQTT traffic to Home Assistant.

1. Add a DNS host override in your router (OPNsense, Pi-hole, etc.):
   - Hostname: **mqtt.us.petlibro.com**
   - IP: **{ha_ip}**
2. Wait for the feeder to reconnect (~30 seconds)
3. Click **Submit** to start listening for the connection

auto_detect_wait.title: Waiting for Feeder Connection
auto_detect_wait.description:
Listening on port {port} for MQTT CONNECT from your Petlibro feeder...

Make sure the DNS override is active. The feeder should reconnect within 30-60 seconds.

auto_detect_confirm.title: Credentials Captured
auto_detect_confirm.description: Successfully captured credentials from your Petlibro feeder!

manual.title: Manual Credential Entry
manual.description: Enter the MQTT credentials captured from your feeder's CONNECT packet.

broker.title: MQTT Broker
broker.description: Enter the MQTT broker details. This should be your local Mosquitto broker that the feeder connects to after DNS redirect.

error.sniffer_timeout: No feeder connection detected. Check DNS redirect and try again.
error.sniffer_port_in_use: Port 1883 is already in use. Stop any MQTT broker first.
